specification.topic-statement: The agent termination system kills a target agent and all of its descendants in a single atomic database transaction, then propagates the dead state into memory and notifies waiting parent agents.

scope.in-scope: Descendant collection, transactional database status updates, audit event recording, in-memory dead-flag propagation, scrollback status messages, parent notification via PostgreSQL NOTIFY, and current-agent switching when the active agent is the kill target.
scope.boundary: Marking agents as dead, inserting audit events, and committing transactions are delegated to the database layer. The schema and transaction guarantees are outside this scope.
How the REPL switches the active agent to the parent after a self-kill is implemented by the agent navigation subsystem. This topic invokes the switch but does not implement it.
The NOTIFY sent to waiting parent agents travels through the PostgreSQL notification channel. The semantics of that channel and how waiting agents consume it are outside this scope.

field.name: recording agent
field.note: UUID of the agent that issued the kill command, not the target

field.name: target
field.note: UUID of the primary kill target

field.name: count
field.note: total number of agents killed, including all descendants and the target

behavior.precondition-checks:
Before the kill begins, the system waits for any in-progress fork operation to complete by polling until a fork-pending flag clears.
If no target is specified, the calling agent kills itself. If the calling agent is the root agent (has no parent), self-kill is refused and a warning is appended to the scrollback.
If a target is specified by identifier, the target is located in the active agent list. If not found, a warning is appended and the command completes without error. If the target is the root agent, the kill is refused and a warning is appended.
behavior.descendant-collection:
All descendants of the target are collected through depth-first traversal. Each agent's children are recursed into fully before the agent itself is added to the collection. This produces a bottom-up ordering: leaf descendants appear before their ancestors.
The collection is bounded at 256 total agents (descendants, excluding the target itself).
behavior.database-transaction:
A database transaction is opened. All the following operations are performed within that transaction. If any step fails, the transaction is rolled back and no database changes are applied.
Each collected descendant is marked as dead in the database, in the order they were collected (bottom-up). The dead status includes a termination timestamp.
The primary target agent is marked as dead in the database.
A single audit event of kind "agent_killed" is inserted into the conversation history of the agent that issued the kill command — not the target. The event carries JSON metadata including the target's identifier, the literal string "user" as the killer, the cascade flag set to true, and the total count of killed agents (descendants plus target).
The target agent's own conversation history receives no record of its termination. The kill event appears only in the history of the agent that issued the command.
The transaction is committed. If the commit fails, the error is returned. All subsequent in-memory changes proceed only after a successful commit.
behavior.memory-propagation:
After the transaction commits, in-memory state is updated.
The target agent's dead flag is set to true. A message describing the termination (including the cascade count) is appended to the target's scrollback. If the target has a parent agent, a PostgreSQL NOTIFY is sent on the parent's notification channel with the payload "dead", to wake any agent waiting for the target to reach a terminal state.
Each descendant has its dead flag set to true and receives a scrollback message. If the descendant has a parent, a NOTIFY is sent to the parent's channel.
NOTIFY is sent only when the database connection is in an idle state (not inside a transaction). Because the commit has already completed at this stage, this precondition is normally satisfied.
If NOTIFY fails after the transaction has committed, the function returns an error even though the database changes are permanent and the dead flags are already set in memory. The kill succeeded; only the wakeup notification was lost.
behavior.active-agent-switch:
After memory propagation, the REPL context is updated to reflect the current navigation state.
If the target was the currently active agent, the REPL switches to the parent agent. A message is appended to the parent's scrollback identifying the terminated agent (by a truncated form of its identifier). If no parent exists at this point, an error is returned.
If the target was not the currently active agent, a message is appended to the active agent's scrollback stating how many agents were killed. No navigation change occurs.
